Okay, this is me a quick one. It's just I wanted to have a little clarification on the point of the questions is that you're trying to get them to say, you know, you're asking all these questions, they've done all these things, and you're like, so what do you think's been the things been holding you back? And that's one of the key questions that you say before you transition to pitch, which is, you know, like, what do you think's been missing, right? And usually they're like, I just, I'm not, I can't stay consistent with it. I don't stick with it. I'm just too lazy. I just like, I need someone to, you know, get me back into it, right? And all those things are code for I need to be held accountable, but you need them to say it. You can't say it. You need them, you need to get them to say it, because then when we propose our solution, it'll totally solve the problem. So what we're doing is leading them to that thought path that gives them the epiphany. The reason they haven't been consistent is because they haven't been held accountable. They haven't had anyone falling off of them in any of the programs they've done in the past, right? And then that is why we'll come back in and solve the problem. Also, just know at the end of last video, I didn't say it overtly, but when I said hey, we can't have this keep going. Like you need to do something today. That's future amplification of pain. So we did the past pain and then we're going future pain. And then that way it's gonna force action, okay? So you need to make sure that you're leading them down the path of explaining that like they just need, what's the thing that's been holding the back? So you summarized everything so you're listening, because you'll use all that stuff in the pitch. Like, okay, so you did all this stuff. What's been holding you back? Why hasn't this worked for you? And then sometimes they won't know that you can lead them to like, so you just need more accountability? You need someone to like really make sure that you follow through with everything? Yes, right? And then they come to that realization. All right, I just wanna make sure just a little clarification, because it's really important. Okay, awesome.
